dict.string: Share Anywhere uses your camera to scan QR codes to connect with nearby devices and to capture photos or videos you want to share. For example, you can scan a QR code displayed on another device to instantly establish a peer-to-peer connection for file sharing.

Share Anywhere accesses your Documents folder to let you select files to share with nearby devices and to save files received from others. For example, you can pick a PDF from your Documents folder and send it wirelessly to a nearby contact.

Share Anywhere uses your local network to discover and connect to nearby devices for direct peer-to-peer file sharing without the internet. For example, it finds other phones on the same Wi-Fi network so you can instantly send photos, videos, or documents to them.

Share Anywhere saves photos and videos received from nearby devices directly to your photo library. For example, when a contact sends you an image over a peer-to-peer connection, it will be saved to your Photos app.

Share Anywhere accesses your photo library so you can select photos and videos to share wirelessly with nearby devices. For example, you can pick multiple photos and send them to a friend's device without using mobile data or the internet.
Share Anywhere needs access to your Apple Music and media library to allow you to select and securely transfer audio files to nearby devices.
Share Anywhere uses location services to help discover nearby devices on the same network for fast file sharing.
